articles.title: HOUSE PRICE ANALYSIS BY COUNTRY: Scotland remains most affordable
articles.summary:
In her latest review of property prices by UK region, Kate Faulkner gives a more detailed analysis of the market in Scotland where it is currently cheapest.  
The post HOUSE PRICE ANALYSIS BY COUNTRY: Scotland remains most affordable appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Scotland has the lowest house prices in the UK, making it the most affordable place to buy a home.  
- A report says that houses there are cheaper than in other parts of the UK.

articles.title: Government restates opposition to rent controls in clear split with Greens
articles.summary:
Greens and Labour clash over rent policy, with Housing Minister Matthew Pennycook claiming caps would make life more difficult for renters.
The post Government restates opposition to rent controls in clear split with Greens appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- The government doesn't want to set limits on how much landlords can charge for rent because they think it could make things harder for people renting homes.  
- The Greens and Labour party disagree about this, with the Greens wanting to have rent controls to help renters.

articles.title: Letting agents plan to raise fees ahead of Renters’ Rights Act
articles.summary:
Many agents will increase the fees they charge landlords, but risk losing them as clients, according to lettings tech firm Goodlord.
The post Letting agents plan to raise fees ahead of Renters’ Rights Act appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Some people who help landlords rent their homes plan to charge them more money soon.
- This might make landlords unhappy and they could decide to find someone else to help them.

articles.title: Rental rules can’t be enforced with £60m, claims leading lawyer
articles.summary:
David Smith, Property Litigation Partner at Spector Constant & Williams, warns funding will not fix chronic shortages of experienced enforcement officers.
The post Rental rules can’t be enforced with £60m, claims leading lawyer appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- A important lawyer thinks that even if the UK government puts £60 million into rental rules, it won't help much because there aren't enough people to make sure those rules are followed.
- There are not enough experienced workers to check if landlords are following the rules, no matter how much money is given to fix the problem.

articles.title: Landlords ‘set to pull more than 200,000 rental properties this year’
articles.summary:
An estimated 220,000 homes will be withdrawn, with 65,000 due to the Renters' Rights Act, according to new research. 
The post Landlords ‘set to pull more than 200,000 rental properties this year’ appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Many people who own rental homes, called landlords, will take over 200,000 of their houses off the market this year.  
- This is happening because new rules are changing how renters can be treated, which is making some landlords decide not to rent anymore.

articles.title: Embarrassment at renting hits at 29, reveals Nationwide
articles.summary:
Millions of people admit they have felt embarrassed about their living situation that sees them sharing with friends, family, or even strangers, according to the Nationwide research.
The post Embarrassment at renting hits at 29, reveals Nationwide appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Many people feel embarrassed about where they live, especially if they have to share their home with friends, family, or even people they don’t know.
- A study found that this feeling often starts around the age of 29.

articles.title: SNP ‘first refusal’ housing proposal comes under fire
articles.summary:
A proposal by Scottish First Minister, SNP leader John Swinney, to give private renters ‘first refusal’ to buy their homes, has come under fire from a property policy expert.
The post SNP ‘first refusal’ housing proposal comes under fire appeared first on Property Investor Today.
articles.ai_summary:
- A Scottish leader wants to let people who rent their homes have the chance to buy them first. 
- Some experts think this idea might not be a good one and are questioning it.

articles.title: England faces historic housing shortfall – households set to surge 17% by 2040
articles.summary:
The number of households in England is projected to rise by 17% to 27.6 million by 2040 causing significant demographic change.
The post England faces historic housing shortfall – households set to surge 17% by 2040 appeared first on Property Investor Today.
articles.ai_summary:
- By the year 2040, there will be about 27.6 million homes needed in England because there will be 17% more families living there.  
- This means that England is going to need a lot more houses, as more people will be living in the country.

articles.title: Northern Ireland’s top emerging investment hotspots
articles.summary:
Northern Ireland’s emerging investment hotspots are delivering compelling opportunities for investors in 2026, with new research from Belfast-based estate agency John Minnis revealing a shift in where landlords are finding the strongest returns.
The post Northern Ireland’s top emerging investment hotspots appeared first on Property Investor Today.
articles.ai_summary:
- Some places in Northern Ireland are becoming popular for people who want to invest in homes, helping them earn money.  
- A report shows that these places will be even better for investors in 2026!

articles.title: Fleet Mortgages launches new BTL 2-year tracker
articles.summary:
Fleet Mortgages, the buy-to-let specialist lender, has today (10th April 2026) announced the launch of three new 75% LTV two-year tracker products available within its three buy-to-let lending ranges: Standard, Limited Company and HMO/Multi-Unit Freehold Block (MUFB).
The post Fleet Mortgages launches new BTL 2-year tracker appeared first on Property Investor Today.
articles.ai_summary:
- Fleet Mortgages has created new loan options for people who want to buy homes to rent out, and they have three different types to choose from.  
- These loans will track interest rates for two years, which means the payments can change based on what the banks decide.

articles.title: Expanding Chestertons Global enters India
articles.summary:
Chestertons Global has expanded its international network into India through a strategic arrangement with BSE-listed Justo Realfintech Limited.
The post Expanding Chestertons Global enters India appeared first on Property Investor Today.
articles.ai_summary:
- A company called Chestertons Global is moving into India to help people with renting and buying homes.  
- They are working together with another company in India to make this happen.

articles.title: Landlords turn to HMOs to boost returns
articles.summary:
A major shift is underway in the UK rental market, with an increasing number of landlords moving to Houses in Multiple Occupations (HMOs).
The post Landlords turn to HMOs to boost returns appeared first on Property Investor Today.
articles.ai_summary:
- Many landlords in the UK are starting to rent out houses to more than one group of people at a time, which is called HMOs.
- They are doing this to make more money from their properties.

articles.title: Yields up but war dents confidence, says Fleet
articles.summary:
Rental yields across England and Wales increased across every region annually in Q1 2026, offering a positive start to the year for landlords, according to Fleet Mortgages’ latest Quarterly Rental Barometer.
The post Yields up but war dents confidence, says Fleet appeared first on Property Investor Today.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords in England and Wales are making more money from renting out homes than last year.  
- But some people are worried about a war that might make things harder for everyone.

articles.title: Auction sales surge past £1.1 billion and staff doubles
articles.summary:
Bond Wolfe’s 50th auction next month will see the Birmingham-based business exceed £1.1 billion in sales in the seven years since its launch in January 2019.
The post Auction sales surge past £1.1 billion and staff doubles appeared first on Property Investor Today.
articles.ai_summary:
- A company called Bond Wolfe is doing really well, selling homes for over £1.1 billion since it started in 2019.
- They are also getting more people to work for them, doubling their staff!

articles.title: New data shows Scotland’s appeal to Gulf buyers
articles.summary:
Scotland is becoming one of the most sought-after UK property markets for Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) buyers, driven by strong price growth, resilient demand and growing international appeal, according to new data released by Sharia’a‑compliant digital
The post New data shows Scotland’s appeal to Gulf buyers appeared first on Property Investor Today.
articles.ai_summary:
- Lots of people from Gulf countries are really interested in buying homes in Scotland because prices are going up and more people want to live there.
- Scotland is becoming a popular place for these buyers because they like what it has to offer.

articles.title: Supply issues as office market thrives
articles.summary:
Despite the brisk market for office accommodation in the Surrey town of Weybridge, a property expert has described it as ‘polarised’.
The post Supply issues as office market thrives appeared first on Property Investor Today.
articles.ai_summary:
- In Weybridge, a town in the UK, lots of people want to rent offices, so the office market is doing well.  
- But there aren't enough offices available for everyone who wants one, which makes it hard for some businesses to find a place to work.
